title:
brain link [--predicate

] [--alias ] [--remove] — --json success output
description: Add, re-alias, or remove an edge between two notes (v2, #331). No --predicate ⇒ the plain SetLink form (a body [[wiki-link]], projected with predicate NULL); --predicate ⇒ the typed CreateRelationship form (a frontmatter `related:` entry — markdown-derived, model A). Grounding resolves <source>/<target> through the tiered id → slug → declared-alias precedence (@atlas/contracts identity keys; the two partial UNIQUE indexes ux_note_links_plain / ux_note_links_pred are the physical enforcement mirror) and refuses (exit 1) an unknown or dirty source/target BEFORE any mutation. Every applied outcome edits the source note and lands EXACTLY ONE commit on refs/heads/main via the canonical mutation order; the projection is re-folded from the committed blob (no LanceDB refresh — links never re-embed). The noop set is exactly: duplicate-identical add, aliasless re-add of an existing edge (an omitted --alias preserves the stored alias), and absent-edge remove — each emits action:"noop", commit:null, noop:true, exit 0, and writes NEITHER git NOR the projection. An alias change on an existing edge is action:"updated" (one in-place mutation, never a new edge). --remove with --predicate p removes exactly that typed edge (the plain link and other predicates survive); without --predicate, only the plain NULL-predicate edge. The commit-null-IFF-noop invariant is machine-enforced by the allOf below.

action.description: added = new plain edge; related = new typed edge; updated = alias CHANGED on an existing edge (a metadata mutation — consumers counting new relationships must not count it); removed = matching edge removed; noop = post-state equals pre-state.

source.description: The RESOLVED source note id (never the raw seed spelling).

target.description: The RESOLVED target note id (never the raw seed spelling).

predicate.description: The relationship predicate; null for the plain SetLink form.

alias.description: The display alias as passed via --alias; null unless --alias (an omitted --alias never clobbers a stored alias).

commit.description: The sha of the ONE commit the applied change landed; null IFF noop.

noop.description: true iff action:"noop" — no git write, no projection write.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Add, re-alias, or remove a plain wiki-link or typed relationship edge between two notes; noop-idempotent; exactly one commit per applied change.

args.name: <source>
args.description: Source note (id, filename slug, or unique declared alias) — the note the edge is written into.

args.name: <target>
args.description: Target note (id, filename slug, or unique declared alias).

flags.description: Typed-relationship predicate: present ⇒ CreateRelationship (frontmatter `related:` entry); absent ⇒ the plain SetLink body wiki-link.
Display alias, meaningful only on add; omitted ⇒ a stored alias is preserved (aliasless re-add is a noop). Refused with --remove (exit 5).

Remove the edge the selector names: with --predicate p exactly that typed edge; without, only the plain NULL-predicate edge. A matching edge ⇒ action:"removed"; no match ⇒ noop (idempotent, never an error).

sideEffects: rewrites the SOURCE note in the working tree (body wiki-link surgery for the plain form; frontmatter `related:` surgery for the typed form)
commits exactly the source note's path onto refs/heads/main (one commit per applied change; unrelated working-tree dirt survives uncommitted)
re-folds the source note's projection rows (notes + note_identity_keys + outgoing note_links) from the committed blob
prohibitedEffects: any git or projection write on a noop (duplicate-identical add, aliasless re-add, absent-edge remove)
clobbering a stored alias on an add whose --alias is omitted
removing an edge the selector does not name (the plain link and other-predicate edges survive a predicate-scoped remove)
re-embedding / any LanceDB write (a link edit never re-embeds)
mutating anything when <source>/<target> is unknown or dirty (grounding fails exit 1 before any apply or commit)

errorCodes.when: --alias combined with --remove, a missing <source>/<target>, or an unknown flag — evaluated before grounding
<source> or <target> resolves to no note (never a noop), or the note is not projected yet
a seed matches more than one note within one identity tier
the source or target note is dirty (uncommitted git diff vs HEAD, or on-disk hash != projection content_hash) — before any apply or commit
the ledger store is absent or unmigrated (link never creates or migrates it)
HEAD is not attached to refs/heads/main (nothing mutated)
another writer holds the vault-maintenance lock
a pre-existing external git index.lock is present at apply time
unexpected internal failure (e.g. the projection edge set disagrees with the clean markdown)
